india.title: American oystercatchers were wiped out in New England, now their numbers are 45% higher along US coasts; here's how
india.summary: Once considered extinct in New England, the American oystercatcher is experiencing a remarkable resurgence thanks to dedicated conservation efforts. These initiatives focus on safeguarding their nesting environments and raising community awareness. Techniques such as banding chicks and preventing disturbances in ground-nesting sites have proven successful. Their resilience, coupled with federal safety measures for related shorebirds, makes their comeback a testament to effective conservation in the face of climate change challenges.

Overall two and three-wheeler sales grew twenty-eight percent during the quarter.

business.title: Dollar wavers as markets grapple with Gulf tensions
business.summary: The US dollar hovered near a one-week high as investors weighed escalating Middle East tensions against hopes for a ceasefire. Concerns over energy supplies supported the greenback, while the yen and euro remained largely steady. The British pound edged higher after UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham reaffirmed his commitment to fiscal discipline, boosting confidence in the country's economic outlook.

business.title: JPMorgan, other US banks set to help finance Japan's $550 billion US investment plan, sources say
business.summary: JPMorgan and other US banks are close to agreeing on financing support for projects under Japan’s $550 billion US investment pledge, according to sources. The move could help Tokyo fulfil commitments made to President Donald Trump, as Japanese banks face challenges in funding large dollar-based infrastructure projects. Japan has announced over $100 billion worth of projects under the scheme.

business.title: Adani Energy Q1 Results: Profit zooms 124% YoY to Rs 1,149 crore; revenue jumps 42%
business.summary: Adani Energy Solutions reported a 124 percent profit increase year-on-year.  Revenue from operations also saw a substantial 42 percent rise during the quarter.  Transmission and distribution segments drove this impressive financial performance.  The smart meter business also demonstrated strong and notable growth.  Overall expenses increased due to higher power purchase and construction costs.

business.title: Gold rises 1% as hopes for US-Iran diplomacy pause oil rally
business.summary: Gold prices gained over 1% as investors tracked diplomatic efforts to ease the US-Iran conflict and assessed the impact on inflation and the Federal Reserve’s rate outlook. Spot gold rose 1.6% to $4,071.59 per ounce, while US gold futures climbed 1.5%. Analysts said bullion is attempting to build a base near the $4,000 level and regain upward momentum.

business.title: Euro zone bond yields tick higher as oil remains elevated; eyes on ECB
business.summary: Euro zone government bond yields are rising as oil prices remain elevated.  This situation threatens inflation outlook and supports tighter European Central Bank policy.  Investors are also watching Britain's gilt market after new Prime Minister Andy Burnham's appointment.  Germany's 10-year bond yield reached its highest level in eight weeks.  The European Central Bank is expected to hold its deposit rate steady this week.

business.title: Julius Baer net new money beats expectations, but de-risking clouds outlook

However, shares of the Swiss private bank fell over 4% as it warned that ongoing de-risking efforts and a revised risk and compliance framework would continue to weigh on growth through 2027.

business.title: Bigger crash ahead? JPMorgan CEO Dimon says he won't buy stocks at current prices, says markets underestimating risks

Dimon also cautioned that massive AI spending may not deliver returns as quickly as markets expect amid escalating Middle East tensions.

business.title: Goldman raises Dutch TTF gas price view on slow Hormuz export recovery
business.summary: Goldman Sachs raised its Dutch TTF gas price forecasts for the final two quarters of 2025, citing expectations of further delays in the normalisation of LNG exports from the Gulf amid heightened Middle East tensions. The investment bank lifted its Q3 and Q4 forecasts to €60 and €53 per MWh, respectively, from earlier estimates of €41 and €40.

business.title: How PayPal went from Wall Street favorite to unwilling merger target
business.summary: PayPal, once a Wall Street favourite and a pioneer in digital payments, is now facing a potential $53 billion takeover bid from Stripe and Advent International. The company’s board is reviewing the offer but reportedly believes the $60.50 per share proposal undervalues the business. PayPal’s market dominance has weakened as rivals like Apple Pay gain ground, despite its strong legacy.

business.title: Schindler Q2 sales miss expectations as weak China market drags
business.summary: Schindler shares fell around 5% as investors worried about weak demand in China’s property market, despite global order growth for a third straight quarter. The elevator maker said new installation orders in China declined over 10%, while demand for modernisation of existing elevators remained strong across markets. CEO Paolo Compagna highlighted growth from the company’s new modular product platform, especially in Europe.

business.title: Choppy oil keeps investors on edge, stocks brace for big earnings week

business.title: Global Market: European shares edge higher as tech, miners lead gains amid earnings focus
business.summary: European shares saw modest gains on Tuesday, driven by technology and mining sectors. Easing oil prices provided a boost to investor sentiment across the continent. Investors closely watched corporate earnings for geopolitical impact on businesses. Technology stocks advanced significantly, anticipating key US company results later this week. Mining shares also climbed, tracking higher commodity prices and adding positive momentum.
